engagements.name: Penetration Testing
engagements.tagline: Adversary-simulated attacks exposing real exploitable paths before attackers find them.
engagements.description: A structured offensive exercise where Mitigence engineers attack your environment using the same techniques as real threat actors. Every engagement is scoped to your environment — results map directly to business risk, not a generic checklist.

phases.name: Scoping & Rules of Engagement
phases.description: Define target systems, test boundaries, emergency notification protocols, and measurable success criteria with your team.

phases.name: Reconnaissance
phases.description: Passive and active information gathering: OSINT, DNS enumeration, network mapping, and service fingerprinting.

phases.name: Exploitation
phases.description: Attempt exploitation of identified weaknesses — including lateral movement, privilege escalation, and data access paths.

phases.name: Post-Exploitation Analysis
phases.description: Assess the full business impact of successful exploitation chains and maximum attainable access.

phases.name: Reporting & Debrief
phases.description: CVSS-rated technical findings report, executive summary, and a structured remediation roadmap delivered in a live debrief.

deliverables: Technical findings report with proof-of-concept evidence
Remediation roadmap prioritised by exploitability and impact
Live debrief session with engineering and leadership teams

engagements.name: Cloud Security Engineering
engagements.tagline: Design and build security controls native to your cloud environment — not retrofitted from on-premises.
engagements.description: Cloud environments introduce security models that differ fundamentally from on-premises infrastructure. Mitigence engineers design, implement, and validate cloud-native security controls across AWS, Azure, and GCP — aligned to your architecture, not a vendor template.

phases.name: Discovery & Current State
phases.description: Map existing cloud architecture, account structures, IAM configurations, network topology, and active workloads.

phases.name: Architecture Review
phases.description: Evaluate design against CIS Benchmarks, CSP security best practices, and your specific threat model.

phases.name: Control Design
phases.description: Design guardrails, detective controls, CSPM integration, and logging strategy tailored to your cloud footprint.

phases.name: Implementation
phases.description: Deploy controls via IaC, configure alerting pipelines, and integrate with existing SIEM or SOAR tooling.

phases.name: Validation & Handover
phases.description: Verify controls fire correctly, document runbooks, and transfer ownership to your engineering team.

deliverables: CSPM policy set tuned to your environment
Handover session with your cloud team

engagements.name: Privileged Access Management
engagements.tagline: Control, audit, and enforce least-privilege access for every privileged account across your estate.
engagements.description: Privileged accounts represent the highest-value targets for attackers. Mitigence designs and implements PAM programmes — from discovery through vault deployment, session recording, and just-in-time access — that give you visibility and control without disrupting operations.

phases.name: Privileged Account Discovery
phases.description: Enumerate all privileged accounts across Active Directory, cloud IAM, service accounts, and application credentials.

phases.name: Policy & Architecture Design
phases.description: Define least-privilege policies, JIT access workflows, and session recording requirements aligned to your risk appetite.

phases.name: PAM Platform Deployment
phases.description: Deploy and configure your chosen PAM platform (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea, or equivalent) to your specification.

phases.name: Integration & Onboarding
phases.description: Onboard target systems, integrate with AD/LDAP and ticketing systems, and configure automated password rotation.

phases.name: Testing & Handover
phases.description: Validate access controls, test emergency break-glass procedures, and train your team on day-to-day operations.

deliverables: Privileged account inventory and risk register
JIT access workflows for critical systems

engagements.name: Third-Party Risk Assessment
engagements.tagline: Systematic evaluation of supplier and partner cyber risk before it becomes your incident.
engagements.description: Your security posture is only as strong as your weakest supplier. Mitigence conducts structured third-party risk assessments — combining questionnaire review, evidence validation, and targeted technical spot-checks — to give you an accurate picture of supply chain exposure.

phases.name: Vendor Scoping & Tiering
phases.description: Classify vendors by data access, system integration depth, and criticality to assign appropriate assessment depth.

phases.name: Questionnaire & Evidence Review
phases.description: Issue and review standardised security questionnaires (based on SIG, CAIQ, or custom frameworks) and supporting evidence.

phases.name: Technical Spot-Check
phases.description: Perform targeted technical validation: OSINT, exposed surface analysis, certificate hygiene, and breach history review.

phases.name: Risk Scoring & Analysis
phases.description: Score each vendor against a consistent risk matrix and identify high-priority remediation or contractual actions.

phases.name: Reporting & Recommendations
phases.description: Deliver a prioritised vendor risk register with recommended contractual, technical, and monitoring actions.

deliverables: Completed assessment evidence packs per vendor

engagements.name: Compliance Program
engagements.tagline: Build and sustain the controls needed to achieve and maintain regulatory certification.
engagements.description: Compliance programmes fail when they treat certification as a destination rather than an ongoing capability. Mitigence designs compliance programmes that satisfy auditors and genuinely reduce risk — mapping controls to multiple frameworks simultaneously where possible to reduce duplicated effort.

phases.name: Gap Assessment
phases.description: Measure current controls against target framework requirements (ISO 27001, SOC 2, NIST CSF, PCI DSS, DORA, or equivalent).

phases.name: Control Mapping
phases.description: Map requirements to controls, identify overlaps across frameworks, and assign ownership for each control domain.

phases.name: Policy & Procedure Development
phases.description: Author or update policies, standards, and procedures to the level of evidence required for audit.

phases.name: Implementation Support
phases.description: Support control owners through implementation — technical, operational, and process-level changes.

phases.name: Audit Preparation & Evidence Pack
phases.description: Compile evidence, conduct internal readiness review, and prepare your team for auditor interviews.

deliverables: Gap assessment report with control heatmap
Audit preparation briefing for key stakeholders

engagements.name: Medical Device Security Assessment
engagements.tagline: Evaluate cyber risk in connected medical devices without disrupting clinical operations.
engagements.description: Connected medical devices introduce unique security challenges — constrained hardware, long lifecycles, patient safety implications, and regulatory complexity under MDR, FDA cybersecurity guidance, and IEC 62443. Mitigence assesses device risk with clinical context, not just a generic vulnerability scanner.

phases.name: Device Inventory & Classification
phases.description: Catalogue all connected medical devices, classify by patient contact, data sensitivity, and network connectivity.

phases.name: Threat Modelling
phases.description: Model realistic attack scenarios per device class — considering both cyber and patient safety impacts.

phases.name: Technical Assessment
phases.description: Non-disruptive technical testing: network traffic analysis, firmware review where accessible, and interface security evaluation.

phases.name: Clinical Impact Analysis
phases.description: Map technical findings to clinical operational risk and patient safety implications with clinical input.

phases.name: Reporting & Remediation Planning
phases.description: Risk-rated report with short-term compensating controls and long-term remediation guidance.

deliverables: Medical device inventory and risk classification
Technical findings report with clinical risk context
Remediation roadmap aligned to device lifecycle

engagements.name: Ransomware Readiness Review
engagements.tagline: Stress-test your defences and recovery capabilities against ransomware attack chains.
engagements.description: Ransomware continues to be the leading cause of significant business disruption. Mitigence evaluates your full defence-in-depth posture — from initial access vectors through to backup integrity and recovery capability — against realistic ransomware attack chains.

phases.name: Attack Path Mapping
phases.description: Map the most probable ransomware entry vectors for your environment: phishing, RDP exposure, VPN vulnerabilities, supply chain.

phases.name: Control Evaluation
phases.description: Assess preventive and detective controls against each attack path: email security, EDR, network monitoring, patch posture.

phases.name: Backup & Recovery Review
phases.description: Evaluate backup architecture, immutability, isolation, and recovery time objectives against realistic ransomware scenarios.

phases.name: Tabletop Exercise
phases.description: Run a facilitated incident scenario to test your team's decision-making and escalation under ransomware conditions.

phases.name: Remediation Roadmap
phases.description: Prioritised roadmap of quick wins and strategic improvements across prevention, detection, and recovery.

deliverables: Control gap analysis across the attack lifecycle
Tabletop exercise report and lessons learned

engagements.name: Identity Security Engineering
engagements.tagline: Modernise identity infrastructure — authentication, authorisation, and lifecycle management.
engagements.description: Identity is the new perimeter. Mitigence designs and implements modern identity security programmes — spanning MFA enforcement, SSO consolidation, identity lifecycle automation, and zero-trust access principles — that reduce attack surface without degrading productivity.

phases.name: Identity Discovery & Risk Assessment
phases.description: Inventory all identity stores, authentication mechanisms, and access patterns — surface orphaned accounts, shared credentials, and MFA gaps.

phases.name: Architecture & Policy Design
phases.description: Design target-state identity architecture: directory consolidation, MFA strategy, conditional access policies, and RBAC framework.

phases.name: MFA & SSO Implementation
phases.description: Deploy and enforce MFA across all user populations; implement SSO to reduce password sprawl and improve visibility.

phases.name: Lifecycle Automation
phases.description: Implement joiner-mover-leaver workflows, automated provisioning/deprovisioning, and access certification processes.

phases.name: Validation & Handover
phases.description: Verify controls are functioning, document operational procedures, and transfer ownership to your identity team.

deliverables: Identity risk assessment and gap analysis

engagements.name: Incident Response Planning
engagements.tagline: Build the plans, playbooks, and capabilities to respond effectively when an incident occurs.
engagements.description: Organisations that plan for incidents before they happen respond faster, communicate better, and recover with less damage. Mitigence builds IR capability from the ground up — practical playbooks, tested escalation paths, and teams who know what to do under pressure.

phases.name: Current-State Review
phases.description: Assess existing IR policies, tooling, team capability, and previous incident history to identify gaps.

phases.name: Playbook Development
phases.description: Build scenario-specific playbooks for your highest-priority incident types: ransomware, data breach, BEC, DDoS, insider threat.

phases.name: Tabletop Exercise
phases.description: Facilitate a realistic scenario exercise with your response team — test decision-making, communication, and escalation.

phases.name: Tooling & SIEM Review
phases.description: Evaluate detection and response tooling against IR requirements; identify gaps in logging, alerting, and forensic capability.

phases.name: Documentation & Sign-off
phases.description: Finalise IR plan, playbooks, RACI matrix, contact trees, and regulatory notification procedures.

deliverables: Incident Response Plan aligned to your organisation
RACI matrix and escalation contact tree
Tabletop exercise report and improvement actions

engagements.name: Security Accreditation Support
engagements.tagline: Expert-led preparation and support through formal security accreditation and certification processes.
engagements.description: Security accreditations — Cyber Essentials, ISO 27001, NIST RMF, FedRAMP, and sector-specific schemes — require precise preparation. Mitigence navigates the technical and procedural requirements alongside your team, reducing time-to-certification and audit surprises.

phases.name: Requirements Analysis
phases.description: Clarify the full scope of accreditation requirements, assess applicability to your environment, and define the certification boundary.

phases.name: Gap Assessment
phases.description: Measure current controls against accreditation requirements and produce a prioritised gap closure plan.

phases.name: Remediation Planning & Support
phases.description: Work alongside your team to close identified gaps — technical, process, and documentation.

phases.name: Evidence Pack Preparation
phases.description: Compile, organise, and quality-check the full evidence pack to auditor submission standards.

phases.name: Accreditor Liaison & Audit Support
phases.description: Support during auditor interviews and technical reviews; manage findings and close-out actions.

deliverables: Gap assessment report with prioritised closure plan
Complete evidence pack to accreditation standard
Policies and procedures to required depth

engagements.name: Network Segmentation
engagements.tagline: Architect and implement network zones that contain breaches and eliminate lateral movement.
engagements.description: Flat networks give attackers unlimited internal reach once they gain a foothold. Mitigence designs and implements network segmentation architectures — zero trust network access, micro-segmentation, and traditional zone-based controls — calibrated to your environment and operational constraints.

phases.name: Discovery & Network Mapping
phases.description: Map current network topology, traffic flows, asset classifications, and existing segmentation controls.

phases.name: Segmentation Architecture Design
phases.description: Design target-state zones and trust boundaries based on asset criticality, data classification, and operational requirements.

phases.name: Firewall & ACL Policy Development
phases.description: Develop detailed firewall rulesets, ACLs, and micro-segmentation policies. Test in a staging environment where possible.

phases.name: Implementation
phases.description: Staged rollout of segmentation controls — minimising operational disruption through phased cutover.

phases.name: Validation Testing
phases.description: Verify segmentation controls function as designed through technical testing and lateral movement simulation.

deliverables: Current-state network topology and asset map
Validation test results confirming segmentation effectiveness

engagements.name: Insider Threat Program
engagements.tagline: Build detection, deterrence, and response capabilities for malicious and negligent insider scenarios.
engagements.description: Insider threats — whether malicious, negligent, or compromised — require a different security approach to external attackers. Mitigence builds insider threat programmes that balance effective monitoring with proportionate, legally sound processes — protecting both the organisation and its people.

phases.name: Risk Assessment & Use-Case Definition
phases.description: Identify the highest-priority insider threat scenarios for your organisation, sector, and data environment.

phases.name: Policy & Legal Framework
phases.description: Develop insider threat policy, define acceptable monitoring scope, and align with employment law and privacy requirements.

phases.name: Detection Use-Case Design
phases.description: Design SIEM and UEBA use cases for anomalous behaviour: data exfiltration, privilege abuse, after-hours access, and account sharing.

phases.name: Tool Deployment & Integration
phases.description: Deploy or tune existing tooling to execute detection use cases; integrate with HR and identity systems for context.

phases.name: Training & Awareness
phases.description: Train your security and HR teams on the programme, escalation procedures, and legal handling of insider incidents.

deliverables: Programme policy and legal framework documentation
Training materials for security and HR teams

engagements.name: Security Assessment
engagements.tagline: A comprehensive baseline evaluation of your organisation's security posture across people, process, and technology.
engagements.description: Before you can improve, you need an honest picture of where you stand. Mitigence's security assessment combines structured interviews, document review, and technical analysis to produce an accurate, unvarnished view of your current security posture — and a practical roadmap for improvement.

phases.name: Scoping & Planning
phases.description: Agree assessment scope, access requirements, stakeholder interviews, and framework alignment (NIST CSF, CIS Controls, ISO 27001).

phases.name: Interview & Document Review
phases.description: Structured interviews with key stakeholders across IT, security, operations, and leadership. Review existing policies and procedures.

phases.name: Technical Review
phases.description: Review technical controls: network architecture, identity management, endpoint posture, logging, vulnerability management, and cloud configuration.

phases.name: Risk Analysis & Scoring
phases.description: Score findings against a consistent risk framework and identify the highest-priority gaps relative to your threat environment.

phases.name: Findings Report & Roadmap
phases.description: Produce a findings report with a prioritised 12-month improvement roadmap, quick-win recommendations, and strategic investments.

engagements.name: Endpoint Protection
engagements.tagline: Deploy and tune endpoint detection, response, and hardening controls across your entire estate.
engagements.description: Endpoints remain a primary attack entry point. Mitigence designs, deploys, and tunes endpoint security programmes — EDR deployment, hardening baselines, application control, and detection tuning — that provide real visibility without alert fatigue.

phases.name: Inventory & Baseline Assessment
phases.description: Inventory all endpoints by type, OS, and current protection status. Measure against CIS hardening benchmarks.

phases.name: Tool Selection & Architecture
phases.description: Select and architect EDR and endpoint protection tooling appropriate to your environment, budget, and team capability.

phases.name: Deployment & Hardening
phases.description: Deploy EDR agents, enforce hardening baselines, disable unnecessary services, and implement application control policies.

phases.name: Detection Tuning
phases.description: Tune detection rules to your environment — minimise false positives, maximise signal quality, and establish response playbooks.

phases.name: Validation & Handover
phases.description: Validate coverage, test detection against known attack techniques (MITRE ATT&CK), and hand over to your security operations team.

engagements.name: OT Security Assessment
engagements.tagline: Assess cyber risk in operational technology environments without disrupting production systems.
engagements.description: OT environments — industrial control systems, SCADA, PLCs, and manufacturing networks — have unique security requirements driven by availability, safety, and legacy architecture constraints. Mitigence assesses OT risk using passive, non-disruptive techniques that won't impact production.

phases.name: Asset Discovery
phases.description: Passive network discovery and asset enumeration using OT-safe techniques (Nozomi, Claroty, or equivalent) — no active scanning of production systems.

phases.name: Architecture Review
phases.description: Review OT/IT network architecture: Purdue model alignment, DMZ design, remote access controls, and wireless exposure.

phases.name: Passive Network Analysis
phases.description: Analyse network traffic to identify unencrypted protocols, unauthorised communications, and anomalous device behaviour.

phases.name: Threat Modelling
phases.description: Model realistic attack scenarios relevant to your sector: ransomware impact on operations, engineering workstation compromise, supply chain intrusion.

phases.name: Reporting & Remediation Planning
phases.description: Risk-rated report with operational context — recommendations respect production constraints and safety requirements.

deliverables: OT asset inventory and network topology

engagements.name: PCI DSS Assessment
engagements.tagline: Scope, assess, and remediate against PCI DSS requirements for cardholder data environments.
engagements.description: PCI DSS compliance requires precise scoping, control implementation, and evidence collection. Mitigence brings both technical and QSA-adjacent expertise to PCI DSS programmes — helping organisations scope correctly, remediate efficiently, and maintain compliance across annual assessment cycles.

phases.name: CDE Scoping
phases.description: Define the cardholder data environment boundary, identify all in-scope systems and data flows, and explore scope reduction opportunities.

phases.name: Gap Assessment
phases.description: Assess current controls against all applicable PCI DSS requirements for your merchant/service provider level.

phases.name: Control Testing
phases.description: Technical testing of controls: network segmentation validation, penetration testing, ASV scanning, and log review.

phases.name: Remediation Support
phases.description: Work alongside your team to close identified gaps and document remediation evidence.

phases.name: Evidence Pack & SAQ/ROC Preparation
phases.description: Compile and quality-check the evidence pack for SAQ self-assessment or QSA audit submission.

deliverables: CDE scope definition and data flow diagrams
Penetration test and ASV scan results
SAQ or ROC supporting evidence pack

engagements.name: Application Security Testing
engagements.tagline: Uncover exploitable vulnerabilities in web, mobile, and API applications through targeted security testing.
engagements.description: Application vulnerabilities are consistently among the leading initial access vectors. Mitigence's application security testing combines automated scanning with deep manual exploitation to identify real vulnerabilities — not just scanner noise — in web applications, APIs, and mobile apps.

phases.name: Application Profiling
phases.description: Understand the application architecture, technology stack, authentication model, and business logic to focus testing effort.

phases.name: Threat Modelling
phases.description: Identify the most likely and impactful attack scenarios specific to your application type and data.

phases.name: Automated & Manual Testing
phases.description: Combine DAST tooling with manual expert testing across OWASP Top 10, business logic flaws, authentication, authorisation, and API security.

phases.name: Exploitation & Impact Assessment
phases.description: Attempt exploitation of identified vulnerabilities to demonstrate real-world impact — not just theoretical severity.

phases.name: Reporting & Developer Briefing
phases.description: Technical findings report with developer-friendly remediation guidance and a live briefing for your engineering team.

deliverables: Findings report with CVSS scores and proof-of-concept evidence
Live debrief with your engineering team
